tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubin, ketones, glucosuria), suggesting significant renal impairment with possible nephrotic features. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria, which may indicate a non-diabetic cause or analytical interference. Lipid profile shows hypercholesterolemia and high LDL. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and further assessment of underlying etiology.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
